html:
KebNi AB ser. B (KEBNI B.ST) | SEK 1.205 | Equity value ~SEK 330M, roughly $35M

KebNi is not the clean “Satcom backlog conversion” story the older drafts wanted it to be. The better thesis is sharper: the market is now punishing KebNi for Satcom delays while ignoring that the real engine has shifted to Swedish defense sensors, Saab NLAW production, and a potentially scalable inertial platform for drones, autonomous systems, and precision defense applications.

In 2025, KebNi did SEK 124.6M of sales, SEK 10.3M of EBITDA, and SEK 8.5M of operating cash flow. Q1 2026 then looked ugly: sales fell to SEK 13.0M and EBITDA was SEK -6.6M, driven by delayed Satcom orders and a temporary component shortage. That Q1 miss is real, but the company also said H1 2026 should still be in line with H1 2025, which was SEK 68.6M of sales. If that is right, Q2 has to be a large recovery quarter, not a slow grind.

The misunderstood asset is the Inertial Sensing business. Saab placed a SEK 134M NLAW IMU order in April 2025, KebNi’s fourth volume order for that program, bringing total Saab IMU volume orders to SEK 348M over 30 months, with deliveries extending beyond 2026. This is not an early-stage prototype. Series deliveries began in mid-2023, and the product is embedded in a live European defense supply chain at a time when anti-armor weapons, autonomous navigation, and NATO-localized procurement all matter more.

The market still sees a lumpy microcap. The variant view is that lumpy is not the same as low quality. KebNi has 24 employees, a high gross-margin sensor mix, and a cost base that can be covered quickly when orders land. Q2 2025 showed the torque: SEK 33.7M of sales, 53% gross margin, 14% EBITDA margin, and SEK 16M of operating cash flow. Q3 2025 was also profitable, with 11% EBITDA margin. The bad Q4/Q1 sequence reset expectations, but it did not invalidate the platform.

Satcom should be treated as option value, not the base case. The ESA LEO Satcom pilot is financially limited, but strategically useful because it validates KebNi’s SOTM terminal in a LEO use case. The company is also developing dual-band Gimbal capability and remains in active customer dialogue, but the evidence today supports “long-cycle upside” rather than “confirmed large backlog.” That distinction matters. The old Satcom-heavy thesis was too aggressive. The new thesis is stronger because investors are paying little for Satcom while the sensor business already has real orders.

SensAItion is the second call option. Its ArduPilot interface approval puts KebNi into a large open-source drone/autonomous vehicle ecosystem, lowering integration friction for UAV makers. KAIST is testing the platform in drone navigation, Aimpoint has a technical collaboration to explore future integration, and the company is seeing drone-related test orders. None of that is yet a volume contract, but this is exactly how defense sensor wins incubate: test unit, integration, qualification, then production order.

At the current price, valuation is not optically screaming cheap on 2026 earnings because Q1 depressed the year. The cheapness is forward. MarketScreener’s visible estimates show roughly SEK 155M of 2026 sales and SEK 200M of 2027 sales, with net income rising from about SEK 10M to SEK 24M. That puts the stock around 30x 2026 earnings but only about 13-14x 2027 earnings, with EV/sales falling toward 1.5x. For a Swedish defense electronics microcap with net cash potential, high gross margins, Saab validation, and Satcom/SensAItion optionality, that is cheap if 2027 is even close.

The balance sheet is acceptable but not bulletproof. Q1 cash was SEK 9.4M, available liquidity including credit lines was SEK 24.4M, and the equity ratio was 66%. The AGM authorized up to 20% issuance, so dilution is the key downside risk if delayed orders consume working capital. The 2026/2029 employee warrant program adds only about 2% dilution if fully exercised, but a rights issue would matter.

Retail context is mixed. The shareholder count jumped from 6,645 at year-end 2024 to 12,452 at year-end 2025, then eased to 11,638 in Q1 2026. Avanza Pension and Nordnet Pension are top holders, and Nordnet/Reddit chatter is focused on “one big order” and potential takeout optionality. That means the stock can move violently on news, but it also means there is a ready retail buyer base if Q2 proves Q1 was timing rather than deterioration. Institutional sponsorship remains thin, which is part of the opportunity.

The best forward setup is simple: if Q2 confirms H1 recovery, the stock stops trading like a failed Satcom story and starts trading like a small Swedish defense sensor supplier with a Saab anchor, drone optionality, and delayed Satcom upside. The current SEK 330M market cap is not discounting much strategic value beyond the known revenue base.

Bear target: SEK 0.60, down 50%. Q2 recovery disappoints, Satcom delays persist, SensAItion stays in test-order mode, and the company uses the 20% issuance authorization at a weak price.

Base target: SEK 2.40, up 99%. 2026 sales recover to SEK 150-170M, 2027 moves above SEK 200M, EBITDA margins normalize into the mid-teens, and the market values KebNi at roughly 2.5-3.0x forward sales for a profitable defense electronics supplier.

Bull target: SEK 5.50, up 356%. Saab follow-on orders continue, SensAItion lands a real UAV or optics volume program, delayed Satcom orders convert, and revenue run-rate moves toward SEK 300-350M with 18-22% EBITDA margins. At that point KebNi becomes a strategic asset, not just a retail microcap.
